questions.question: What games or sports do you play for fun?
questions.correct_answer: Games or sports played for enjoyment

questions.question: Which games or sports do you play for competition?
questions.correct_answer: Games or sports played to win a trophy or achieve a goal

questions.question: What does 'friendly matches' mean?
questions.explanation: Matches where the focus is on having fun rather than winning.

questions.question: Who is your opponent in a game?
questions.explanation: Someone you play against or compete with in a game.

questions.question: What is the tally of marks?
questions.correct_answer: (ii) record of points in a game/match
questions.explanation: A record of points scored during a game or match.

questions.question: What is sporting spirit?
questions.correct_answer: (ii) being fair and respecting each player in a game
questions.explanation: The quality of being fair, respectful, and honest in a game.

questions.question: Why did Prabhat not want to play games he might lose?
questions.explanation: Prabhat felt terrible when he lost, so he avoided playing games where he might lose.

questions.question: What was special about the last Friday of each month?
questions.correct_answer: The coach conducted friendly matches in the games period.
questions.explanation: The coach organized friendly matches on this day to promote sportsmanship and fair play.

questions.question: Who was responsible for maintaining the score for the match?
questions.explanation: Prabhat was given the responsibility of keeping score during the match.

questions.question: Why did Prabhat not feel happy about winning?
questions.correct_answer: He had won unfairly by cheating.
questions.explanation: Prabhat felt guilty for winning unfairly, which affected his happiness.

notes.title: Importance of Sportsmanship
notes.content: Fair play and respect for others are essential in sports and games.

notes.title: Types of Games or Sports
notes.content: Games or sports can be played for fun, competition, or both.

notes.title: Meaning of 'Friendly Matches'
notes.content: Friendly matches are games where the focus is on having fun rather than winning.

notes.title: Opponent in a Game
notes.content: An opponent is someone you play against or compete with in a game.

notes.title: Tally of Marks
notes.content: A record of points scored during a game or match.

notes.title: Sporting Spirit
notes.content: The quality of being fair, respectful, and honest in a game.

notes.title: Prabhat's Problem with Losing
notes.content: Prabhat felt terrible when he lost, so he avoided playing games where he might lose.

notes.title: Importance of Fair Play
notes.content: Fair play and respect for others are essential in sports and games.

notes.title: Types of Competition
notes.content: Games or sports can be played for fun, competition, or both.

notes.title: Consequences of Cheating
notes.content: Cheating can lead to feelings of guilt and affect one's happiness.

facts.fact: Prabhat always wanted to win and had trouble accepting defeat.
The coach conducted friendly matches on the last Friday of each month.
Surya was an excellent badminton player who won the game with ease.
Prabhat cheated to win the match and felt guilty about it later.
The focus of friendly matches is on having fun rather than winning.
An opponent in a game is someone you play against or compete with.
A tally of marks is a record of points scored during a game or match.
Sporting spirit involves being fair, respectful, and honest in a game.
Prabhat's behavior changed after he cheated to win the match.
Surya did not feel bad about losing and was a good sport.
